registration_requirement: Do not run a confirmation episode until an immutable external registration URL and timestamp have been added to the final protocol.
purpose: Test pilot-verified routing on environment code maintained outside this repository and compare it with cost-matched deployment rules.

risk_events: failure to reach the goal before truncation
low terminal value under adverse arrivals

pre_confirmation_requirements.environment_provenance: Pin package versions or source commits and record licenses before adapter development ends.
pre_confirmation_requirements.policy_provenance: Freeze every fallback and candidate checkpoint or deterministic policy before generating confirmation tasks.
pre_confirmation_requirements.split_discipline: Use disjoint development, calibration, pilot, and final seeds. Confirmation task parameters must be hashed before any confirmation reset.
pre_confirmation_requirements.candidate_screen: Use development tasks only and retain at most one positive-mean candidate per external domain.
pre_confirmation_requirements.proposal_rule: Use the existing five-neighbor support-aware proposal with all feature transforms frozen before confirmation.
pre_confirmation_requirements.no_outcome_adaptation: Do not change task membership, policies, proposal rules, pilot rules, score weights, or analysis after any confirmation pilot or final outcome.

cost_matched_baselines: uniform pilot allocation with the same total candidate-plus-fallback episodes
random promotion among proposals matched to the verified promotion count

pilot_design.multiplicity_rule: Bonferroni over the complete fixed proposal family.
pilot_design.batch_count_rule: After the proposal family size m is frozen, set B=ceil(log2(m/0.05)) so a unanimous exact sign result can cross the Bonferroni threshold.
pilot_design.budget_curve: Report every prefix and every subset of the B batches where computationally feasible; this analysis is pre-specified for the external study.
pilot_design.pilot_final_separation: Pilot and final seeds must be disjoint and recorded in the final protocol.

score_sensitivity.requirement: Define domain scores and the complete coefficient grid before any confirmation reset. Hold deployed routes fixed when reporting score sensitivity.

primary_inference.estimand: Equal-external-domain mean relative improvement of pilot-verified routing over the fixed domain fallback.
primary_inference.resampling: Resample external domains with replacement and tasks within each sampled domain; also report fixed-domain stratified intervals for comparison.
primary_inference.randomization: Task-level sign flips nested within external domain, preserving equal domain weight.
primary_inference.domain_scope: The three named external domains are fixed benchmark cases, not a random sample of deployed applications.

reporting_commitment: Report all fixed external tasks, proposals, pilot outcomes, accepted routes, cost-matched baselines, score variants, and the result regardless of direction.
